data.body: PASS-1 review note (rubric T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture, OVERLAY): No T1 bright-line fires for the scorer-registry + dataset-schema plan. The plan extends an existing, mature eval substrate (src/rebar/llm/eval.py with validate_scorer/validate_eval_spec/at_least/coverage/kappa/to_junit, Inspect AI + promptfoo/JUnit interop already considered in this very ticket's post-mortem) rather than introducing a novel architecture. A name->pure-function dispatch table is a well-trodden, conventional pattern needing no prior-art search. No external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, perf target, or migration is introduced, and no unverified capability assertion is made (the plan names the concrete existing functions it will extend). T1 = clean.
